definition: Commodity input costs are a contributing (not primary) driver of equity earnings cycles. AI capex creates novel physical supply constraints: each GW of data center capacity requires 4-8 kt copper, 150-200 TWh incremental US electricity by 2028, and 3-5 Bcf/d incremental gas demand. Commodity regime determines P/E impact: disinflationary = expansion, inflationary >10% YoY = 3-6 turn compression.
evidence: Commodity-regime-dependent P/E relationship survives across four decades with 8/10 confidence
Current mixed commodity environment supports but does not expand ~21-22x multiple
Output gap and financial accelerator more explanatory than commodities for equity cycles
open_questions: Whether physical supply constraints become binding bottleneck for AI capex
Whether shift to rising commodity prices would be the primary supply-side threat to margins
Timing of commodity cost impact on earnings
